# Native SDK soundboard example

A music-library browser built to showcase native-rendered Native SDK UI: an album grid with real cover art through the runtime image pipeline, track lists with native context menus, a now-playing bar with real audio playback, transport controls and a live seek slider, and search with the field's built-in clear affordance. The custom light/dark theme follows the system appearance live — there is no in-window theme control.

**The catalog is real data.** `src/music_manifest.zon` is a committed manifest generated by `tools/prepare-example-music.sh`; the model imports it as a typed value and flattens the albums and tracks at comptime. Albums carry a variable number of tracks, so each album records where its run starts and how long it is — nothing assumes a fixed stride.

**Playback is real audio.** Pressing play issues `fx.playAudio` for the track's file through the runtime audio effect family; the load acknowledgment (with the decoded duration), coarse position ticks, the one completion at natural end, and failures all arrive as typed messages through the ordinary update path. The audio files themselves are gitignored — run `tools/prepare-example-music.sh` to place them under `assets/music/`. Without them, a play attempt degrades honestly: the now-playing bar shows "music assets not prepared — run tools/prepare-example-music.sh", browsing keeps working fully, and the deterministic test suite passes with no audio files present.

## Authoring split (markup-first)

- `src/header.native` — brand, library tabs (the `<tabs>` buttons lower to segmented triggers, so the active tab lifts per the house treatment), and the search field (its trailing clear is the component's own).
- `src/nowplaying.native` — the transport bar: circular cover (the avatar image binding is the one image reference markup carries), title/artist (doubling as the status surface for the assets-not-prepared notice), prev/play/next, seek slider, time labels, queue badge.
- `src/view.zig` — the Zig-only sections the closed markup grammar deliberately excludes: rounded-square cover images, the grid's column count, scaled paragraph headings, and per-track native context menus (Play Next, Copy Title). The root view composes the compiled markup sections and the Zig sections into one tree, so widget identity, dispatch, and theming behave exactly as in a single-source view.
- `src/model.zig` — the typed manifest import and its comptime flattening, playback/search/queue/appearance state, the controlled scroll offsets (each region's `on_scroll` stores the applied offset and the view echoes it back, so a rebuild mid-gesture never resets a list), and `update`.
- `src/theme.zig` — a custom "studio" token set (warm neutrals, violet accent, softer radii) over the built-in light/dark themes; high-contrast requests fall back to the framework palettes and reduce-motion zeroes the motion tokens.

Both `.native` files compile at comptime (`canvas.CompiledMarkupView`); the test suite additionally builds them through the runtime interpreter and asserts engine parity.

## Cover art

The committed covers in `src/art/` are 512px JPEGs, one per album, named by the manifest's `art` slots and embedded at comptime. Live macOS decodes them through the platform codec seam; the null platform's strict test decoder speaks only the deterministic PNG subset, so under `-Dplatform=null` registration fails and every album degrades to its initials fallback — the honest codec-less-host state the suite pins. `init_fx` registers the covers at boot; a failed decode leaves that album on initials, never a broken boot.

## Fixed capacities

- The manifest's albums and tracks, flattened at comptime (variable tracks per album).
- One registered cover image per album, of the runtime's 16 image slots.
- 16-entry play-next queue; a full queue drops the request and counts it.
- 48-byte search buffer.
- Two context-menu items per track row: the all-songs list mounts every catalog track as a row, comfortably inside the 512-item per-view budget.
- The widget tree peaks in the all-songs list (one row per catalog track), held under 768 nodes of the 1024 per-view budget by a test.

## Run

```sh
tools/prepare-example-music.sh   # once, from the repo root: prepares the gitignored audio from your local library
native dev
```

Right/ctrl-click a track row for the native context menu; Copy Title pipes the title through `pbcopy`. The theme follows the system appearance live — flip the OS to dark and the studio palette follows.

Run the deterministic suite (dispatch and playback through the fake effects executor, the cover decode/register/draw path, theming, markup engine parity, layout budgets — hermetic, no audio files needed):

```sh
native test -Dplatform=null
```

Verify live through the automation harness:

```sh
native build -Dautomation=true
./zig-out/bin/soundboard &
native automate assert 'gpu_nonblank=true' 'role=listitem name="Exit Signs by Harbor Sleep"' 'role=button name="Play or pause"'
```
